<source_slice path="src/main.c" lines="112-142">
112:       "  -V, --version             show the version;\n"
113:       "  --build-configuration     show jq's build configuration;\n"
114:       "  -h, --help                show the help;\n"
115:       "  --                        terminates argument processing;\n\n"
116:       "Named arguments are also available as $ARGS.named[], while\n"
117:       "positional arguments are available as $ARGS.positional[].\n");
118:   }
119:   exit((ret < 0 && code == 0) ? 2 : code);
120: }
121: 
122: static void die() {
123:   fprintf(stderr, "Use %s --help for help with command-line options,\n", progname);
124:   fprintf(stderr, "or see the jq manpage, or online docs  at https://jqlang.github.io/jq\n");
125:   exit(2);
126: }
127: 
128: static int isoptish(const char* text) {
129:   return text[0] == '-' && (text[1] == '-' || isalpha(text[1]));
130: }
131: 
132: static int isoption(const char* text, char shortopt, const char* longopt, size_t *short_opts) {
133:   if (text[0] != '-' || text[1] == '-')
134:     *short_opts = 0;
135:   if (text[0] != '-') return 0;
136: 
137:   // check long option
138:   if (text[1] == '-' && !strcmp(text+2, longopt)) return 1;
139:   else if (text[1] == '-') return 0;
140: 
141:   // must be short option; check it and...
142:   if (!shortopt) return 0;
</source_slice>

<source_slice path="tests/shtest" lines="309-326">
309: # --args, --jsonargs, $ARGS.positional
310: $VALGRIND $JQ -n -c --args '$ARGS.positional' foo bar baz > $d/out
311: printf '["foo","bar","baz"]\n' > $d/expected
312: cmp $d/out $d/expected
313: $VALGRIND $JQ -n -c --jsonargs '$ARGS.positional' null true '[]' '{}' > $d/out
314: printf '[null,true,[],{}]\n' > $d/expected
315: cmp $d/out $d/expected
316: $VALGRIND $JQ -n -c '$ARGS.positional' --args foo 1 --jsonargs 2 '{}' --args 3 4 > $d/out
317: printf '["foo","1",2,{},"3","4"]\n' > $d/expected
318: cmp $d/out $d/expected
319: $VALGRIND $JQ -n -c '$ARGS.positional' --args --jsonargs > $d/out
320: printf '[]\n' > $d/expected
321: cmp $d/out $d/expected
322: 
323: ## Regression test for issue #2572 assert when using --jsonargs and invalid JSON
324: $VALGRIND $JQ -n --jsonargs null invalid && EC=$? || EC=$?
325: if [ "$EC" -ne 2 ]; then
326:     echo "--jsonargs exited with wrong exit code, expected 2 got $EC" 1>&2
</source_slice>

<source_slice path="tests/setup" lines="1-44">
1: #!/bin/sh
2: 
3: # This is meant to be included by each test's shell script driver.
4: 
5: if [ -n "$TRACE_TESTS" ]; then
6:     set -x
7: fi
8: 
9: set -eu
10: 
11: JQTESTDIR=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)
12: JQBASEDIR=$JQTESTDIR/..
13: JQ=${JQ:-$JQBASEDIR/jq}
14: 
15: if [ -z "${NO_VALGRIND-}" ] && which valgrind > /dev/null; then
16:     VALGRIND="valgrind --error-exitcode=1 --leak-check=full \
17:                        --suppressions=$JQTESTDIR/onig.supp \
18:                        --suppressions=$JQTESTDIR/local.supp"
19:     VG_EXIT0=--error-exitcode=0
20:     Q=-q
21: else
22:     VALGRIND=
23:     VG_EXIT0=
24:     Q=
25: fi
26: 
27: mods=$JQTESTDIR/modules
28: 
29: clean=true
30: d=
31: clean () {
32:     if ! $clean; then
33:         echo "See temp files in $d!"
34:     elif [ -n "$d" ]; then
35:         rm -rf "$d"
36:     fi
37: }
38: trap clean EXIT
39: d=$(mktemp -d -t jqXXXXXX || true)
40: if [ -z "$d" ]; then
41:     echo "Your OS does not support mktemp(1) -d" 1>&2
42:     exit 1
43: fi
44: 
</source_slice>

## ChangeLog:966-970
966: 	Travis CI automatically detects when a commit has been made and pushed
967: 	to a GitHub repository that is using Travis CI, and each time this
968: 	happens, it will try to build the project and run tests. This includes
969: 	commits to all branches, not just to the master branch. When that
970: 	process has completed, it will notify a developer in the way it has been

54: dnl Running tests with Valgrind is slow. It is faster to iterate on

56: dnl fix leaks.

58:    AS_HELP_STRING([--disable-valgrind],[do not run tests under Valgrind]))

40: else
41: BUILT_SOURCES = src/builtin.inc src/config_opts.inc src/version.h
42: .y.c:
43: 	$(AM_V_YACC) echo "NOT building parser.c!"
44: .l.c:

46: endif

<source_slice path="README.md" lines="1-60">
1: # jq
2: 
3: `jq` is a lightweight and flexible command-line JSON processor akin to`sed`,`awk`,`grep`, and friends for JSON data. It's written in portable C and has zero runtime dependencies, allowing you to easily slice, filter, map, and transform structured data.
4: 
5: ## Documentation
6: 
7: - **Official Documentation**: [jqlang.github.io/jq](https://jqlang.github.io/jq)
8: - **Try jq Online**: [jqplay.org](https://jqplay.org)
9: 
10: ## Installation
11: 
12: ### Prebuilt Binaries
13: 
14: Download the latest releases from the [GitHub release page](https://github.com/jqlang/jq/releases).
15: 
16: ### Docker Image
17: 
18: Pull the [jq image](https://github.com/jqlang/jq/pkgs/container/jq) to start quickly with Docker.
19: 
20: ### Building from source
21: 
22: #### Dependencies
23: 
24: - libtool
25: - make
26: - automake
27: - autoconf
28: 
29: #### Instructions
30: 
31: ```console
32: git submodule update --init # if building from git to get oniguruma
33: autoreconf -i               # if building from git
34: ./configure --with-oniguruma=builtin
35: make -j8
36: make check
37: sudo make install
38: ```
39: 
40: Build a statically linked version:
41: 
42: ```console
43: make LDFLAGS=-all-static
44: ```
45: 
46: If you're not using the latest git version but instead building a released tarball (available on the release page), skip the `autoreconf` step, and flex or bison won't be needed.
47: 
48: ##### Cross-Compilation
49: 
50: For details on cross-compilation, check out the [GitHub Actions file](.github/workflows/ci.yml) and the [cross-compilation wiki page](https://github.com/jqlang/jq/wiki/Cross-compilation).
51: 
52: ## Community & Support
53: 
54: - Questions & Help: [Stack Overflow (jq tag)](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/jq)
55: - Chat & Community: [Join us on Discord](https://discord.gg/yg6yjNmgAC)
56: - Wiki & Advanced Topics: [Explore the Wiki](https://github.com/jqlang/jq/wiki)
57: 
58: ## Li… [truncated 81 bytes]

<source_slice path="Makefile.am" lines="90-140">
90: endif
91: 
92: ### Error injection for testing
93: 
94: if ENABLE_ERROR_INJECTION
95: lib_LTLIBRARIES += libinject_errors.la
96: libinject_errors_la_SOURCES = src/inject_errors.c
97: libinject_errors_la_LIBADD = -ldl
98: libinject_errors_la_LDFLAGS = -module
99: endif
100: 
101: ### Building the jq binary
102: 
103: # Remake the version.h header file if, and only if, the git ID has changed
104: .PHONY: .FORCE
105: .FORCE:
106: generate_ver = ver="`{ $(srcdir)/scripts/version || echo '$(VERSION)' ; } | sed 's/.*/\#define JQ_VERSION \"&\"/'`"
107: .remake-version-h: .FORCE
108: 	@ $(generate_ver); test "x`cat src/version.h 2>/dev/null`" = "x$$ver" || touch .remake-version-h
109: src/version.h: .remake-version-h
110: 	mkdir -p src
111: 	$(AM_V_GEN) $(generate_ver); echo "$$ver" > $@
112: src/config_opts.inc:
113: 	mkdir -p src
114: 	$(AM_V_GEN) if test -x ./config.status; then \
115: 	  ./config.status --config; \
116: 	else echo "(unknown)"; \
117: 	fi | sed -e 's/\\/\\\\/g' -e 's/"/\\"/g' -e 's/^/"/' -e 's/$$/"/' -e 's/^/#define JQ_CONFIG /' > $@
118: src/main.c: src/version.h src/config_opts.inc
119: 
120: src/builtin.inc: $(srcdir)/src/builtin.jq
121: 	mkdir -p src
122: 	$(AM_V_GEN) sed -e 's/\\/\\\\/g' -e 's/"/\\"/g' -e 's/^/"/' -e 's/$$/\\n"/' $(srcdir)/src/builtin.jq > $@
123: src/builtin.o: src/builtin.inc
124: 
125: CLEANFILES = src/version.h .remake-version-h src/builtin.inc src/config_opts.inc
126: 
127: bin_PROGRAMS = jq
128: jq_SOURCES = src/main.c src/version.h
129: jq_LDFLAGS = -static-libtool-libs
130: jq_LDADD = libjq.la -lm
131: 
132: if WIN32
133: jq_LDADD += -lshlwapi
134: endif
135: 
136: if ENABLE_ALL_STATIC
137: jq_LDFLAGS += -all-static
138: endif
139: 
140: ### Tests (make check)
</source_slice>

`Makefile.am` defines the jq binary target:

103: # Remake the version.h header file if, and only if, the git ID has changed
104: .PHONY: .FORCE
105: .FORCE:
106: generate_ver = ver="`{ $(srcdir)/scripts/version || echo '$(VERSION)' ; } | sed 's/.*/\#define JQ_VERSION \"&\"/'`"

108: 	@ $(generate_ver); test "x`cat src/version.h 2>/dev/null`" = "x$$ver" || touch .remake-version-h
109: src/version.h: .remake-version-h

111: 	$(AM_V_GEN) $(generate_ver); echo "$$ver" > $@
112: src/config_opts.inc:
113: 	mkdir -p src
114: 	$(AM_V_GEN) if test -x ./config.status; then \

117: 	fi | sed -e 's/\\/\\\\/g' -e 's/"/\\"/g' -e 's/^/"/' -e 's/$$/"/' -e 's/^/#define JQ_CONFIG /' > $@
118: src/main.c: src/version.h src/config_opts.inc
